title: 10 best herbal creams for bad breath

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: The tea tree oil in these creams has potent antimicrobial properties that help kill bacteria and germs in the mouth, reducing bad breath caused by microbial overgrowth.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal tea tree creams may contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and reduce gum inflammation, which is a common cause of bad breath.
Antioxidant properties: Tea tree oil's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to bad breath by reducing oxidative stress in the mouth.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with bad breath because of its antibacterial properties. The active compound, melaleuca oil, contains terpenes that inhibit the growth of oral bacteria, such as Streptococcus mutans and E. coli, which contribute to bad breath. By reducing the bacterial load in the mouth, tea tree creams help to eliminate the source of bad breath, promoting fresher breath and a healthier oral environment. Regular use can lead to improved oral health and confidence.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Cineole has antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, thereby alleviating bad breath.
Terpinen-4-ol: Terpinen-4-ol has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that help soothe mouth sores and reduce bacteria that can cause bad breath.
Linalool: Linalool has antiseptic properties that help combat the growth of bacteria and fungi in the mouth, thereby reducing bad breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Essential oil extracted from the leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia is commonly used in creams for bad breath due to its antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves also contain compounds like cineole, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects that help combat bad breath.
Leaves: The leaves' essential oil can also help freshen breath by reducing bacteria growth in the mouth, contributing to its use in creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia from a pesticide-free area to ensure quality.
Steam distill the leaves at 100-150 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours to extract essential oil.
Combine 20 grams of the extracted essential oil with 80 grams of emulsifying wax in a double boiler.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of vitamin e oil into the mixture and heat until melted.
Add 10 grams of distilled water to the mixture and stir until it reaches a consistent cream texture.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial agents: Peppermint creams contain antimicrobial compounds that help kill bacteria that cause bad breath by reducing their growth and eliminating them from the mouth and gums.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint cream can reduce swelling and irritation in the mouth and gums, which can contribute to bad breath, and also soothe gum inflammation caused by gum disease.

Refreshing and deodorizing: Peppermint's invigorating and cooling properties can stimulate saliva production, which helps neutralize acids and wash away bacteria that cause bad breath, while its deodorizing effects can leave the mouth feeling fresh and clean.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol present in peppermint has the ability to kill bacteria in the mouth, particularly in the gum crevices and between teeth, which are common sources of bad breath. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can help stimulate saliva production, which helps to neutralize acid and wash away bacteria, further reducing bad breath. Regular use of peppermint creams can promote a fresher, healthier mouth.
remedy_constituents: Terpenes (specifically, menthol and menthone): These volatile compounds have antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, contributing to bad breath.

Phenolic compounds (specifically, rosmarinic acid and carvacrol): These antioxidants have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce the proliferation of bacteria and inflammation in the mouth that can cause bad breath.
Carboxylic acid (specifically, menthone): This compound has a numbing effect on the mucous membranes in the mouth, helping to reduce the sensation of bad breath and provide temporary relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the primary part used due to their high content of menthol and menthone, which provide antibacterial properties to help combat bad breath.
Stems: Stems of Mentha x piperita also contain menthol and menthone, which are used in creams for their deodorizing and antibacterial properties.
Roots: Roots of the plant contain menthone and other compounds that contribute to their use in creams for bad breath, although they are less commonly used than leaves or stems.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30g of dried m x piperita leaves and flowers in 500ml of carrier oil at 60°c for 2 hours.
Strain the oil mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes of pressing.
Combine 10g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler and melt at 40°c for 10 minutes.
Gradually add 50ml of the infused oil to the melted wax mixture and stir at 30°c for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a mold and let it set at room temperature for 30 minutes before unmolding.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Purple coneflower's antimicrobial compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, help kill bacteria in the mouth and throat that can cause bad breath.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in purple coneflower creams reduce swelling and redness in the gums and tongue, which can contribute to bad breath.
Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and lead to an overgrowth of bacteria that causes bad breath.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with bad breath because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The active compounds in Echinacea, such as alkylamides and polysaccharides, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can help alleviate bad breath. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties can help combat the bacteria that cause bad breath. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Echinacea purpurea creams can help promote a healthy oral environment and fresher breath.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, specifically echinacoside, have antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to bad breath.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, such as echinacin, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, which can lead to bad breath.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that can help protect the mouth from damage and reduce the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bad breath-causing bacteria.
Leaves: The leaves contain essential oils and compounds that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect oral tissues.

Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea contain flavonoids and other compounds that have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and fight off bad breath-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the summer months when the plant is in full bloom 1 to 2 cups.
Dry the echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 100°f for 2 to 3 hours.
Infuse 1 cup of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in 4 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and mix it with 1 cup of beeswax and 1 cup of shea butter in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to a container and labeling it for use within 6 months.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol, combat bacteria and other microorganisms responsible for causing bad breath (halitosis) by inhibiting their growth and killing them off.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to bad breath, while also helping to protect against gum disease and other oral health issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with bad breath because of its antimicrobial properties. The active compound thymol in thyme has been shown to effectively combat bacteria in the mouth, including those responsible for halitosis. Thyme creams can be applied to the tongue, gums, and teeth to reduce bacterial growth, thereby eliminating the source of bad breath. Additionally, thyme's deodorizing properties help to eliminate volatile sulfur compounds, which contribute to bad breath. Regular use can lead to fresher breath and a healthier oral environment.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, thereby decreasing the production of volatile sulfur compounds responsible for bad breath.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol exhibits antimicrobial activity that inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi in the mouth, leading to a reduction in the causes of bad breath.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the mouth and prevent the formation of volatile compounds that contribute to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in oils with antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria causing bad breath.
Essential oil from Leaves: The essential oil extracted from thyme leaves contains compounds like thymol, which is effective in reducing oral bacteria.
Flowers: Thyme flowers have similar antimicrobial properties to the leaves, which help prevent bacterial growth that leads to bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the early morning for optimal potency.
Infuse 10 grams of the harvested thymus vulgaris in 100 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 100 grams of coconut oil and 10 milliliters of the herbal extract in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes and allow it to cool before whipping into a creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Marjoram's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and borneol, help eliminate bacteria that cause bad breath by inhibiting their growth and reproduction.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marjoram's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath by preventing the formation of biofilms that harbor odor-causing bacteria.

Antioxidant properties: Marjoram's antioxidant compounds, such as carnosol, neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to bad breath by promoting gum disease and other oral health issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Origanum majorana, also known as sweet marjoram, creams helps with bad breath because of its antimicrobial properties that help to combat the bacteria responsible for halitosis. The antibacterial agents present in sweet marjoram creams can effectively reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, thereby eliminating the source of bad breath. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can soothe and calm the oral tissues, promoting a healthy mouth environment that helps to prevent bad breath. remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: This phenolic compound has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, thereby eliminating the primary cause of bad breath.
Thymol: As a phenolic terpene, Thymol has potent antimicrobial and antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of microorganisms responsible for bad breath, keeping the mouth fresh and clean.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that help combat the oxidative stress and bacterial overgrowth in the mouth, leading to fresher breath and a reduced risk of bad breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antimicrobial properties, which help to kill bacteria responsible for bad breath.
Flowers: They are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and protect the mouth and gums.
Stems: They are used for their astringent properties, which help to reduce inflammation and dry out the bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh origanum majorana leaves and flowers in the morning.
Dry the harvested origanum majorana in a single layer at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1/2 cup of dried origanum majorana with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to set.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Sage's antimicrobial compounds, such as thujone and camphor, help combat bacteria and other microorganisms in the mouth that contribute to bad breath.
Antioxidant Properties: Sage's antioxidants, like rosmarinic acid, neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and cause bad breath by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

The active compounds in sage, particularly camphor and thujone, help combat oral bacteria that cause bad breath, or halitosis. Additionally, sage's antimicrobial properties reduce inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to bad breath. remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: This monoterpenic phenol has antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth that contribute to bad breath.
Linalool: As a terpene, linalool exhibits antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat oral pathogens and inflammation that can lead to bad breath.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antibacterial and antiseptic properties, which help reduce bacteria that cause bad breath.
Roots: They are used for their antimicrobial properties, which help fight off bacteria and other pathogens responsible for bad breath.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried salvia officinalis leaves in the morning when essential oils are most concentrated.
Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried leaves in 8 ounces of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container after 2 weeks of maceration.
Mix the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the cooled mixture with a hand mixer until light and fluffy for about 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemongrass creams contain compounds like citral and geraniol, which exhibit antimicrobial activity against bacteria that cause bad breath, reducing their growth and eliminating odors.

remedy_constituents: Citral: A naturally occurring terpene with antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria that contribute to bad breath.
Limonene: A monoterpenic compound with antibacterial properties, effective in inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath, such as Streptococcus and Escherichia.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in citral and geraniol, which are responsible for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to eliminate bad breath-causing bacteria.

remedy_recipe: Harvest cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning when the plant is at its highest concentration of citral.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Create a decoction by steeping 1 part dried cymbopogon citratus leaves in 4 parts distilled water for 30 minutes.
Mix the decoction with 20% beeswax and 80% coconut oil in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and becomes a smooth, even cream.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary's antimicrobial compounds help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause bad breath by reducing their growth and eliminating them from the mouth and gums.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the mouth and gums, which can contribute to bad breath by minimizing inflammation and promoting a healthy oral environment.

The essential oils in rosemary, such as carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria in the mouth, including those that cause bad breath. Additionally, rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce gum inflammation and promote a healthy mouth environment, further contributing to fresher breath and overall oral health. remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: This phenolic compound exhibits antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath.

Rosmarinic acid: As a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory, rosmarinic acid helps to reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, preventing the formation of volatile sulfur compounds that contribute to bad breath.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams for bad breath because of their antibacterial properties that help reduce oral bacteria.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning for maximum essential oil content.
Steep 1 cup of rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Combine 1 cup of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of glycerin and 2 ounces of beeswax in a saucepan.
Add 2 teaspoons of the rosmarinus officinalis infusion to the saucepan and stir until the beeswax melts.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 30 minutes before pouring into containers.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath by reducing the production of saliva and causing gum irritation.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds found in ginger, including gingerol and sesquiterpenes, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath, such as Streptococcus mutans and Fusobacterium nucleatum.

Digestive stimulant properties: Ginger's ability to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea can help reduce halitosis by reducing the presence of stomach acid in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with bad breath because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The active compound, gingerol, has been shown to reduce plaque formation and inhibit the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which are common causes of bad breath. Additionally, ginger's warming and soothing effects can help to reduce gum inflammation and promote a healthy oral environment, making it an effective remedy for bad breath and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath.

remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These are the most commonly used part of ginger due to their high content of essential oils, which are responsible for their antibacterial properties and ability to combat bad breath.
Roots: The roots of ginger are also used to make creams for bad breath as they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to soothe and heal oral infections.

remedy_recipe: Infuse 5 grams of dried zingiber officinale in 100ml of coconut oil for 2 hours at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Combine 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add the infused zingiber officinale mixture to the beeswax and shea butter mixture stirring until combined.
Allow the mixture to cool and harden for at least 30 minutes before scooping into containers.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Ironwort creams contain antimicrobial compounds that help kill bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause bad breath, reducing their ability to produce volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) that contribute to halitosis.

Stems: Sideritis scardica stems are used due to their antimicrobial properties, which help kill bacteria responsible for causing bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh sideritis scardica leaves by cutting them with scissors in early summer morning.
Dry the harvested leaves in a dark place at 30 degrees celsius for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 10 grams of dried leaves with 100 grams of petroleum jelly in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes with constant stirring until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and allow it to cool down completely before capping and labeling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with bad breath because its essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, have natural antibacterial and antifungal properties. These compounds help to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause halitosis (bad breath), as well as eliminate fungal infections in the mouth. Additionally, English lavender's soothing and calming effects can also alleviate gum inflammation and irritation, further contributing to fresher breath and a healthier oral environment.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Lavender's antimicrobial activity helps to reduce the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that cause bad breath, such as streptococcus, staphylococcus, and E. coli.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers from lavandula angustifolia plants for optimal potency.
Dry the lavender flowers using a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Combine 20% dried lavender flowers with 80% sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for infusion and extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and add beeswax at a ratio of 10:1 to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with bad breath because they contain compounds that have natural antibacterial properties, which help to eliminate bacteria in the mouth responsible for causing halitosis. The anethole present in fennel also reduces inflammation and irritation in the gums and tongue, which can contribute to bad breath. Additionally, fennel's digestive benefits can help alleviate gum disease and other oral health issues that often lead to bad breath. Decongestant and expectorant properties: Fennel's natural decongestant and expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and promoting healthy digestion, which in turn can help alleviate symptoms of halitosis (bad breath).

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce inflammation in the mouth, throat, and gums, which can contribute to bad breath by creating an environment conducive to bacterial growth and VSC production.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and dry them in the shade for 24 hours.
Grind dried foeniculum vulgare leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of powdered foeniculum vulgare with 10 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander creams helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh coriandrum sativum leaves and clean them thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned coriandrum sativum leaves in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours to reduce moisture content.
Combine one tablespoon of dried coriandrum sativum powder with two tablespoons of coconut oil and three drops of peppermint essential oil in a small bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the coriandrum sativum powder is fully incorporated into the coconut oil.
Transfer the cream to a sterilized glass jar, label it clearly, and store it in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature of 40 degrees fahrenheit for up to 14 days.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers and clean them thoroughly in cold water.
Infuse one cup of dried calendula flowers in two cups of boiling water for ten minutes.
Combine the infused liquid with eight ounces of cold-pressed olive oil and heat it gently for thirty minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken completely at room temperature over six hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties, which effectively combat the underlying causes of halitosis. Additionally, wormwood's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, further alleviating symptoms of bad breath. By tackling these root causes, wormwood creams provide a natural solution for freshening breath and promoting oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood's antimicrobial compounds, such as absinthin and arteannuin, help kill bacteria and other microorganisms in the mouth, reducing the causes of bad breath.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds, like camphor and borneol, reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and gums, which can contribute to bad breath.
Antioxidant properties: Wormwood's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and lead to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wormwood are used to create a calming and soothing effect, helping to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat that can contribute to bad breath.
Barks: Wormwood's barks contain compounds that have antibacterial properties, making it effective against the bacteria that cause bad breath by inhibiting their growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest artemisia absinthium leaves in abundance for a minimum of 250 grams.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until crispy.
Combine 150 grams of dried artemisia absinthium with 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax.
Melt the mixture over low heat for 15-20 minutes stirring occasionally to combine ingredients.
Strain the cooled mixture into a glass jar using cheesecloth or a coffee filter for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with bad breath because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The gel-like substance found in aloe vera has been shown to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can help alleviate symptoms of bad breath. Additionally, the antibacterial compounds present in aloe cream have been proven to combat the bacteria that cause halitosis, leaving your mouth feeling fresh and clean. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, aloe creams provide a natural solution for combating bad breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the mouth, which can contribute to bad breath by reducing the production of saliva that helps neutralize odor-causing bacteria.

Antibacterial property: The antibacterial agents present in aloe vera cream have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Streptococcus mutans and E. coli, which are common causes of bad breath (halitosis).

Soften and protect mucous membranes property: Aloe vera's soothing properties help to soften and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, tongue, and throat, reducing inflammation and irritation that can lead to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing compounds like salicylic acid and sulfur, which help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause bad breath.
Rhyzomes: Rich in antimicrobial properties, rhyzomes can effectively eliminate odorous volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) responsible for bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from 3-4 leaves of organic aloe barbadensis weighing approximately 200 grams.
Combine 150 milliliters of distilled water with 15 grams of dried peppermint leaves and simmer in a double boiler for 20 minutes.
Strain the peppermint mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl removing excess liquid and solids.
Mix 100 grams of aloe vera gel with 30 milliliters of coconut oil and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil in a stainless steel mixing bowl.
Pour the combined herbal cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at 40 degrees fahrenheit for up to 6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant creams helps with bad breath because of its potent antibacterial properties, which target the underlying causes of halitosis. The creams contain a unique compound called curcumin, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to bad breath. Additionally, curry plant's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce gum inflammation and promote healthy gums, further combating bad breath. By applying these creams to the affected areas, users can experience a significant reduction in bad breath and fresher, cleaner mouth sensations.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal curry plant creams contain compounds that inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and viruses, helping to reduce the causative agents of bad breath (halitosis).
Anti-inflammatory properties: The creams' anti-inflammatory properties can soothe and reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, which is often associated with bad breath.

Antioxidant properties: Curcumin, a key component of curry plant extracts, has antioxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress and damage to oral tissues, contributing to fresher breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Curry plant roots have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to combat bad breath and other oral health issues due to their antimicrobial properties.
Barks: The barks of the curry plant contain flavonoids, which have antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried helichrysum italicum flowers and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Combine the harvested helichrysum italicum flowers with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 4 weeks shaking the jar daily to infuse the oils with the herb's properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 20% of the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a double boiler until fully melted and cooled to create a smooth creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Myrtle creams helps with bad breath because they contain the essential oils of the myrtle plant, which have natural antibacterial properties. These properties help to combat the bacteria that cause bad breath by reducing their growth and activity. Additionally, myrtle creams often include other ingredients like tea tree oil and peppermint oil, which also possess antibacterial and deodorizing properties. Together, these ingredients work synergistically to provide a fresh and clean mouth, eliminating unpleasant odors and leaving your breath smelling sweet and minty.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Myrtle's antimicrobial compounds help combat the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause bad breath, reducing the production of volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) responsible for halitosis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Myrtle's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce inflammation in the oral cavity, which can contribute to bad breath by irritating gum tissue and disrupting the natural balance of oral flora.

Antioxidant properties: Myrtle's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and disrupt the mouth's natural defense mechanisms, potentially contributing to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the myrtle plant are valued for their antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacteria responsible for bad breath.
Barks: Myrtle bark is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation in the mouth and eliminating bad breath.
Seeds: Myrtle seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh myrtus communis leaves in a warm place for at least two weeks or until crispy.
Grind dried myrtus communis leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of ground myrtus communis powder with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of coconut oil in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oils have fully infused the herb extract.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar and let cool to room temperature before storing.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for bad breath are topical treatments that help eliminate foul-smelling breath by addressing the underlying causes, such as gum disease, dry mouth, or poor oral hygiene. The best herbal creams for bad breath include Mentha x piperita, Eucalyptus globulus, Zingiber officinale, Cymbopogon citratus, Syzygium aromaticum, Cinnamomum verum, Cinnamomum zeylanicum, Melaleuca alternifolia, Salvia officinalis, and Thymus vulgaris. If left untreated, bad breath can significantly impact one's social life, lead to feelings of embarrassment, and even affect job opportunities, causing anxiety and stress.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bad breath that are treated with herbal creams are acne, eczema, and psoriasis. These conditions can cause skin lesions, inflammation, and irritation, leading to discomfort and embarrassment. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, chamomile, and aloe vera have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help soothe and heal affected areas, reducing redness, itching, and scaling.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with bad breath is a blend of peppermint, tea tree, and eucalyptus. Peppermint oil's antimicrobial properties reduce bacteria that cause bad breath, while tea tree oil's antiseptic properties combat germs and inflammation. Eucalyptus oil's decongestant properties help to clear sinuses and reduce postnasal drip, which can contribute to bad breath. When applied topically to the gums and tongue, this trio works synergistically to promote fresh breath and a healthy oral environment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with bad breath because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol content in peppermint oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath, such as Streptococcus and Escherichia. Additionally, the oil's cooling sensation helps to numb the palate and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to bad breath. As a result, using Mentha x piperita creams can help to eliminate the underlying causes of bad breath and leave the mouth feeling fresh and clean.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps with bad breath by temporarily numbing the nerve endings in the mouth, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with oral infections and conditions.
Eugenol: It helps with bad breath by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause halitosis (bad breath).
Limonene: It helps with bad breath by having antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of oral infections and conditions that contribute to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of peppermint are commonly used because of their high concentration of menthol, which has a strong antimicrobial effect and helps to reduce bad breath.
Stems: The stems of peppermint contain menthol and other compounds that have antibacterial properties, which help to kill bacteria in the mouth responsible for bad breath.
Roots: The roots of peppermint contain menthone, which has a refreshing and deodorizing effect, making it useful in creams for bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of fresh leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes to create a strong tea.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Combine 1 tablespoon of the infused tea with 1 tablespoon of glycerin and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil.
Gradually add the tea mixture to the beeswax and coconut oil mixture, stirring constantly for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and set for at least 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Eucalyptus globulus, such as eucalyptol, have been shown to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to bad breath. Additionally, eucalyptus oil's decongestant properties can help alleviate sinus congestion, which can also be a cause of bad breath. By targeting the root causes of bad breath, Eucalyptus globulus creams can provide a natural and effective solution to freshen breath and promote oral health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as a natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing the growth of bacteria in the mouth that contribute to bad breath.
Eucalyptol: Exhibits decongestant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to bad breath caused by respiratory issues.

Catechin: Displays potent antimicrobial activity, inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause bad breath, particularly those associated with gum disease and mouth infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are commonly used due to their high oil content, particularly eucalyptol, which has antibacterial properties to combat bad breath.
Barks: Eucalyptus globulus barks are utilized for their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and reduce bad breath caused by gum inflammation.
Buds: Eucalyptus globulus buds are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing bad breath and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves using a solvent like ethanol in a ratio of 1:2.
Combine the extracted liquid with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Melt the mixture at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit for 10-15 minutes until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Add 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of sweet almond oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with bad breath because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the gums and mouth. The active compound, gingerol, has antimicrobial properties that combat bacteria responsible for bad breath. Additionally, ginger has a warming effect that can stimulate saliva production, which helps wash away bacteria and food particles that contribute to bad breath. This makes Zingiber officinale creams a popular natural remedy for alleviating halitosis. Regular use may also promote gum health and overall oral well-being.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have antibacterial properties, which help reduce the number of bacteria in the mouth that can cause bad breath.
Shogaols: They possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and tongue, contributing to bad breath.
Curcuminoids: These compounds have potent antioxidant properties, which can neutralize free radicals and reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause bad breath.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain compounds with antimicrobial properties that help combat the bacteria causing bad breath.
Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the mouth.
Stems: The stems also contain antimicrobial compounds that help control the growth of bacteria in the mouth, thereby reducing bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Grate 5-7 grams of dried zingiber officinale roots into a bowl for later use.
Combine 20 grams of distilled water with 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until beeswax melts completely.
Add 5-7 grams of grated zingiber officinale roots to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams helps with bad breath because they contain the essential oils of lemongrass, which have natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce the bacterial growth in the mouth, which can cause bad breath. The lemongrass oil also helps to soothe and calm the mouth and throat, reducing irritation and inflammation that can contribute to bad breath. By controlling bacterial growth and soothing the mouth, Cymbopogon citratus creams can provide relief from bad breath.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Citral, a terpene, helps by reducing the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath, thereby improving oral hygiene.
Geraniol: Geraniol, a terpene, has antibacterial properties that help in killing the bacteria responsible for bad breath, providing relief.
Limonene: Limonene, a terpene, exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing gum inflammation and thereby reducing the likelihood of bad breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high citral content, which has antimicrobial properties that help combat bad breath-causing bacteria.
Rhyzomes: Used for their oil, which is rich in geraniol and linalool, compounds that have antibacterial properties and contribute to fresh breath.
Stems: Used for their oil as well, which contains compounds like geraniol and linalool, helping to reduce bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of cymbopogon citratus dried leaves and powder them into a fine consistency.
Infuse 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil with 50 grams of powdered cymbopogon citratus for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil using a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids.
Blend 500ml of the infused oil with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Pour the blended mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum creams helps with bad breath because of its antibacterial and antifungal properties. The active ingredient, caryophyllene, found in clove oil, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause bad breath. When applied topically, Syzygium aromaticum creams can help reduce the bacterial load in the mouth, relieving symptoms of bad breath. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of the cream may help soothe and calm oral tissues, promoting a healthier oral environment and fresher breath.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: Eugenol has antibacterial properties, which help eliminate the bacteria that cause bad breath by inhibiting their growth and killing them.
Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, thereby preventing irritation and infection that can lead to bad breath.
Linalool: Linalool has antimicrobial properties, which help control the growth of microorganisms in the mouth, including bacteria and fungi, that contribute to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Buds: They are used to make creams for bad breath because of their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce plaque and gum inflammation.
Fruits: They are used to make creams for bad breath because of their antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the formation of volatile sulfur compounds that cause bad breath.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for bad breath because of their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of syzygium aromaticum flowers at dawn when dew is still present on the petals.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 14 days.
Infuse the dried flowers in 100ml of coconut oil at 70°c for 30 minutes.
Mix the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 25g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10 minutes or until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum creams helps with bad breath because of its antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat the bacteria responsible for halitosis. The active compound, cinnamaldehyde, is known to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans and other oral pathogens, thereby reducing the production of volatile sulfur compounds that cause bad breath. By targeting the root cause of the issue, Cinnamomum verum creams provide a natural and effective solution for individuals struggling with persistent bad breath. Regular use can lead to fresher breath and improved oral health.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound has antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, a primary cause of bad breath.
Lignans: Lignans in Cinnamomum verum have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that contribute to bad breath.
Eugenol: Eugenol has antibacterial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria in the mouth and reduce their ability to cause bad breath.
remedy_parts: Barks: Cinnamomum verum barks are used due to their high content of cinnamic aldehyde, which has antimicrobial properties that help combat bad breath.
Leaves: Cinnamomum verum leaves are used because they contain essential oils with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce bad breath and gum inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Cinnamomum verum rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of cinnamic aldehyde, which is effective in reducing bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of cinnamomum verum oil and 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax and cinnamomum verum oil together in the double boiler for 30 minutes.
Add 200 milliliters of distilled water and 50 grams of glycerin to the mixture and stir well.
Continue heating the mixture for 20 minutes or until the desired consistency is achieved.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the herbal cinnamomum verum cream cool and set.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum creams helps with bad breath because of its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria in the mouth. The active compound, cinnamaldehyde, has been shown to reduce the levels of volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) responsible for causing bad breath. Additionally, cinnamomum zeylanicum has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe gum inflammation and reduce irritation. By targeting the root causes of bad breath, these creams can provide effective relief and promote fresher breath.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound has antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath.
Cinnamyl acetate: It exhibits antifungal properties, which help control the growth of fungi that can lead to oral infections causing bad breath.
Catechin: This flavonoid has antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, thereby minimizing the likelihood of bad breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their essential oil content, which contains compounds like eugenol and cinnamaldehyde that have antimicrobial properties.
Barks: Used due to their high concentration of cinnamaldehyde, a compound that has been shown to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Used due to their essential oil content, which contains compounds like cinnamaldehyde and linalool that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250ml of carrier oil such as jojoba oil in a saucepan with 20g of cinnamomum zeylanicum bark for 4 hours.
Combine the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 20g of candelilla wax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture until the beeswax and candelilla wax have melted completely in about 10 minutes.
Whisk in 10g of vitamin e oil and 5g of preservative and continue stirring for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with bad breath because of its antimicrobial and antibacterial properties. The active compound, tea tree oil, found in these creams effectively kills the bacteria responsible for causing bad breath. By reducing the bacterial load in the mouth and on the teeth, these creams create an environment where oral health thrives. Furthermore, tea tree oil's deodorizing properties also help eliminate volatile sulfur compounds, contributing to fresher breath and an overall healthier oral environment.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Helps with bad breath by reducing inflammation and bacterial growth in the mouth, thus preventing the spread of halitosis-causing bacteria.
Terpinen-4-ol: Helps with bad breath by exhibiting antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms responsible for causing bad breath.
Cymene: Helps with bad breath by showcasing its antimicrobial properties, which help reduce bacterial populations in the mouth and alleviate bad breath symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used in creams because they contain the highest concentration of tea tree oil, a natural antibacterial agent that helps combat bad breath.
Buds: Used in creams because they are rich in antimicrobial properties, which help reduce bacteria that cause bad breath.
Stems: Used in creams because they contain a combination of compounds that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and protect the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of shea butter to melt in a double boiler.
Combine 50 grams of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil with 20 grams of vitamin e oil.
Mix the essential oil and vitamin e oil into the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture.
Add 200 grams of distilled water to the mixture and stir for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a mold and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams helps with bad breath because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds like carnosic acid, ursolic acid, and rosmarinic acid, which have been shown to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth. These compounds also help to soothe and heal gum inflammation, reducing the likelihood of bad breath. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Salvia officinalis can help to reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, promoting a healthy environment for oral hygiene.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: They help reduce bad breath by inhibiting the growth of bacteria in the mouth and throat, which can cause halitosis.
Ursolic acid: It has antimicrobial properties that help combat the bacteria responsible for causing bad breath, thereby reducing its occurrence.
Bornyl acetate: This terpene helps reduce bad breath by its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the oral mucosa.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of antioxidants and antibacterial properties, which help reduce oral bacteria and freshen breath.
Seeds: Utilized for their astringent and antibacterial properties, which aid in reducing gum inflammation and preventing bad breath.
Flowers: Employed for their pleasant fragrance and antimicrobial properties, contributing to the overall freshness and oral health benefits of the creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh salvia officinalis leaves from a sunny spot in your garden.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Chop 10 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Steep the chopped dried leaves in 100 milliliters of olive oil at 40 degrees celsius for 48 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and store in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams helps with bad breath because of their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound thymol in these creams has been shown to reduce the growth of oral bacteria, which are often responsible for halitosis. By killing these bacteria, thymol helps to eliminate the sources of bad breath. Additionally, thymol's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce redness and swelling in the mouth and gums, further contributing to fresher breath and overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol helps to combat bad breath by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause halitosis.
Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, preventing bad breath caused by gum disease and other oral infections.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid exhibits antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals in the mouth that contribute to bad breath and gum disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antimicrobial and antifungal properties to combat bacteria that cause bad breath.
Flowers: Used for their volatile oils that have antibacterial properties to freshen breath.
Stems: Used for their essential oils that have antiseptic properties to reduce plaque and fight germs causing bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30g of dried thyme leaves in 100ml of carrier oil such as jojoba oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the oil infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 10g of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the infused oil to the melted wax and butter mixture stirring constantly to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.
